title: Verbesina crocata
taxonomy: Verbesina crocata is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is a subclass of the larger group known as flowering plants, or angiosperms. Verbesina crocata is also known to belong to the genus Verbesina and the tribe Heliantheae. This classification helps to group Verbesina crocata with other similar species.
uses: Verbesina crocata is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. In some regions, Verbesina crocata is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. Its roots and leaves are often used in herbal remedies to alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory infections. Furthermore, Verbesina crocata is also used to treat skin conditions, including wounds and eczema.

side_effects: Verbesina crocata, also known as golden crownbeard, is a plant used in traditional medicine, but its use may lead to some side effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been reported to cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, when consumed. In some cases, the plant may also cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory problems.
